briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for July 20, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Intensification of the Climate Crisis and Its Widespread Impacts
sections.content: Globally, the deepening climate crisis is having widespread impacts. In Spain, consecutive wildfires amidst a 40-degree Celsius heatwave have burned an area half the size of Seoul [4], while in Japan, 60% of sandy beaches are at risk of disappearing by 2100 [5]. Domestically, migratory pests from China, caused by abnormal weather, have put the southern coastal regions on high alert [8], and Napa Valley has shown the two faces of the climate crisis, oscillating between extreme drought and heavy rainfall [6]. Direct impacts on human life are also evident, with climate change disrupting people's sleep [25, 32], and specifically in India's Gujarat region, high nighttime temperatures are causing an annual loss of 81 hours of sleep [38]. Furthermore, climate change disproportionately affects women [26], threatens livestock farming [33] and traditional crafts [34], and research suggests it could be a major driver of increased waterborne diseases in the future [37]. Amidst these crises, the United States is grappling with extreme weather, while former President Trump is reportedly ignoring the urgency of climate change [24], and it has also been pointed out that the country is simultaneously facing multiple crises, including budget cuts for essential government agencies and new vulnerabilities to environmental disasters [40].

sections.title: 🌍 International Community's Perception of Climate Change and Policy Challenges
sections.content: 기후 변화에 대한 대중의 인식은 엇갈리는 양상을 보입니다. 한 연구에 따르면 대다수의 미국 유권자가 극심한 날씨를 기후 위기와 연관 짓고 있으나 [22, 39], 동시에 약 4분의 1은 기상 재해가 기후 변화와 관련이 있다고 생각하지 않는 것으로 나타났습니다 [35]. 이러한 인식의 차이 속에서 기후 변화와 넷제로에 대한 여론조사가 진행 중이며 [29], 기후 변화에 적응해야 한다는 목소리와 함께 [23] 시간을 낭비하지 말고 재생에너지로의 전환을 가속화해야 한다는 촉구가 이어지고 있습니다 [28]. 또한, 한 유기농 농부는 기후 변화의 빠른 속도에 준비할 시간이 부족하다며 실망감을 표했으며 [30], 칼럼을 통해 기후 변화의 열기가 우리를 구워 죽이고 있으니 행동해야 한다는 강력한 경고도 나왔습니다 [27]. 한편, 연기금들은 최악의 지구 온난화 시나리오에 대처하기 위해 노력하고 있으며 [36], 기후 변화 영향 연구를 위한 8개월 탐사가 프랑스에서 진행되는 등 [31] 국제 사회는 다양한 방식으로 기후 위기에 대응하려 하고 있습니다.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Republic of Korea's Acceleration of Carbon Neutrality and Renewable Energy Transition
sections.content: The Republic of Korea is making various efforts to achieve carbon neutrality goals and accelerate the transition to renewable energy. The government has announced plans to increase renewable energy capacity by more than six times by 2040 [16], and a policy direction emphasizing eco-friendliness in semiconductor measures has also been presented [21]. Active movements for carbon neutrality are being observed across the country. Examples include Suncheon City's operation of 'Carbon Moa Tantane' [9], Gyeonggi Newspaper's first step towards a carbon-neutral communication system [10], and Yangpyeong County's basic plan for carbon neutrality and green growth, including practical training [12]. The Saemaul Undong Taebaek City Council's climate change campaign [2], Gurye County Rural Leaders Association's efforts for environmental preservation and carbon neutrality [14], and Seongbuk-gu's simultaneous promotion of heatwave response and carbon neutrality [15] demonstrate active participation from local communities and civic organizations. In the corporate sector, Sampyo Cement is strengthening its carbon-neutral management through the publication of its 2025 ESG report [13], and Yesan County is operating an emergency management system for school meal safety nets to respond to abnormal weather conditions [3], illustrating that climate action is being concretized across various sectors. Furthermore, the World Heritage Committee is discussing cooperation to counter climate change and armed conflict, with the possibility of a 'Busan Declaration' being mentioned [1], hinting at Korea's potential for international contributions.

sections.title: 🌱 Future Climate Action Through Innovation and Cooperation
sections.content: Innovative technological development and international cooperation for responding to the climate crisis are also steadily progressing. Unusual battery technologies utilizing molten salt for renewable energy storage and even human sweat have been introduced [17], and analyses of the 'butterfly effect' brought about by the transition to electric vehicles are ongoing [7]. In the education and research sector, Jeju National University successfully concluded a capstone program combining renewable energy and hyper-scale AI, solidifying its position as a global AI talent platform [18]. International policy changes are also being observed. Germany is revising its Renewable Energy Act [19], and in-depth discussions are taking place on ways to achieve carbon neutrality in energy-vulnerable regions [11]. Notably, joint efforts towards sustainable development goals are being strengthened through international cooperation, such as the Gyeonggi Council for Sustainable Development's training delegation holding talks with the Nepal Renewable Energy Confederation [20].

briefing.closing: As discussed in today's briefing, we must remember that the climate crisis is a global challenge, but also an opportunity for innovation and cooperation. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.title: Majority of US voters link extreme weather to climate crisis, study finds
articles.description: Top Democrat says findings show public ‘way ahead of the politicians’ as Trump dismisses global heating as ‘hoax’Amid a summer of dangerous heat, drought and floods, a majority of Americans are connecting increasingly severe weather to the climate crisis, new polling shows, despite efforts by Donald Trump to dismiss global heating as a “con job” and a “hoax”.It’s a sign that attempts to suppress polarize climate concerns may not be seeing full success, said Grace Adcox, senior climate strategist

summary.phenomenon: During a summer marked by dangerous heatwaves, droughts, and floods, new polling indicates that a majority of American voters are linking increasingly severe weather events to the climate crisis.
summary.outlook: This suggests that attempts to suppress or polarize public concerns regarding climate issues are not fully succeeding.
